page: Microsoft Azure E96bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Vector Neural Network Instructions. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable and optimized for high-performance block storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with the passmark CPU score and stress-ng multi-core metrics placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test reaching the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is also strong, registering 329.85 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 135.97 GB/sec for writes. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50% average category.

The resource profile of this instance makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, as demonstrated by a static web serving throughput of 63.12 GB/sec and 86.58 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. For artificial intelligence workloads, the server handles small-model prompt processing efficiently at 2502.23 tokens/sec. However, a significant tradeoff exists for larger models; text generation using a 70B LLM model drops to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec, indicating that large-scale model inference is constrained without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E96bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is optimized for Premium Block Storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-threaded decompression and memory bandwidth, alongside a top 10% tier ranking in cryptographic encryption. Single-core performance remains average. The server is highly effective for database operations, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. However, it exhibits a clear performance tradeoff in large language model text generation, where throughput drops to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec for 70B models due to the absence of hardware accelerators.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96bs_v5 is an EBSv5 virtual machine featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and high block storage performance, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic benchmark results.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96bs_v5 is an EBSv5 VM with 96 Intel vCPUs and 672 GB RAM, optimized for block storage and memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-memory, block-storage-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six Intel vCPUs and robust multi-core performance for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory.
Optimized for Premium Block Storage with no local storage.
Top 10% tier performance in cryptographic encryption benchmarks.
Strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth.
Weak performance for large-scale LLM text generation.
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving.
